MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MARIA SULAIMANI's Motion To Set Aside.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is continued to November 2, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. to be heard in Dept. 206, which issued the March 5, 2020 dismissal order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Off Calendar

Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Setting Aside Dismissal, And Motion To Strike The Plaintiff'S Complaint; Or In The Alternative Motion To Dismiss; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jim D. Newman, Esq. (818-863-8304; jnewman@hanover.com) of The Hanover Law Office for Defendants.
No other appearances.

DISCUSSED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FASTY FRESH, INC.'s Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Setting Aside Dismissal, And Motion To Strike The Plaintiff'S Complaint; Or In The Alternative Motion To Dismiss; And Request For Monetary Sanctions:

Defendants' motion for reconsideration of the order setting aside dismissal of this case is granted. Upon reconsideration, plaintiff's motion to set aside the dismissal is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff's motion was granted largely because no opposition brief was filed, but defendants make a convincing showing that they would have opposed the motion had they known about it. No sanctions are awarded to anyone.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR No. 9711, Email: lreding@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)
